english: na, frq. 2. if I may say so, so to speak, as it were; *dí-la cǐ zer* (di la ci zer) what is this called? frq., also without la; to make a noise, e.g. *sag sag zér-wa* (sag sag zer ba) C. to foam with a hissing noise, to sparkle, of wine, beer; *zér-mkan* (zer mkhan) 1. he that is saying. 2. W., said, called, mentioned, esp. for the older źes byá-ba (zhes bya ba). — *zér-ke'* (zer skad) C. rumour, report. — *zér-p̌og-c̀an* (zer phog can) W. speaking in an uncivil or offensive manner. — *zer-ri* (zer ri) C. rumour. — 2 to drive in, nails, v. gzér-ba (gzer ba).
small chip, šiṅ-zél (shing zel) wood-shavings W.

I. num. figure: 142. II. imp. of zá-ba (za ba). III. sbst. resp. sku-zó (sku zo), = lus-kyi kams (lus kyi khams) physical constitution, sku-zo mdog légs-la (sku zo mdog legs la) as the appearance of your majesty's bodily constitution is so excellent Glr.; zo bzáṅ-ba (zo bzang ba) a good complexion Cs. — 2. figure, delineation, representation, perh. better to be spelt bzo (bzo) (?) — 3. mould, zo-čágs (zo chags) showing mouldy spots Sch. (?); zo-már (zo mar) old, mouldy butter, so prob. S.g.; zo-ša (zo sha) Lt. mouldy meat
1. sbst., pail, bucket, šiṅ - zo (shing zo) wooden pail, ču-zo (chu zo) water-pail. — 2. vb. v. bzó-ba (bzo ba).

1. deceit, fraud, falsehood (Lex. = rdzub (rdzub)), zóg-c̀an (zog can) 1. lying, deceitful; liar W. 2. adulterate, counterfeit W.; zog-ldán (zog ldan), zóg-po (zog po) Cs. id., zog-méd (zog med) the opp.; sgyu-zóg (sgyu zog) (religious) hypocrisy Pth.; čos-zóg (chos zog) priestcraft Mil.; *zol-zóg* (*zol zog*) = zog (zog) W. — 2. vulg. pronunciation in C. and W., inst. of the following.

(vulgo zog (zog)) 1. ware, merchandise, goods, zoṅ - min - smár (zong min smar) not goods but ready money Lex.; rgyágs-zoṅ (rgyags zong) goods taken by travellers along with them to be bartered for provisions; smán-zoṅ (sman zong) drugs; tsóṅ-zoṅ (tshong zong) merchandise Cs.; zóṅ-rnams rnám-pa sna-tsógs (zong rnams rnam pa sna tshogs) goods of all kinds; *zóg-gi dág-po* (*zog gi bdag po*) Ts. owner of the goods, master of the estate, heir, = nór-bdag (nor bdag). — 2. Sch. worth, price(?). — 3. Sch. doubt(?). — 4. Sch. lie(?).

attention, heed, care, gen. zon byéd-pa (zon byed pa), to pay attention, to take heed, to beware, dgrá-la (dgra la) of an enemy Pth.; also c. accus. Mil.; zon sdig-pa spoṅ mi śes (zon sdig pa spong mi shes) seems to mean: not knowing the attention needful for renouncing sin Thgy.; zon-méd (zon med) heedless; zon - grábs (zon grabs) provision, precaution, preventive measure Sch.
Ts., stuff- or woolen shoes; *bob-zón* (*bob zon*) id., covered with leather.
Ts., *zob-zób jhé'-pa* (*zob zob jhe' pa*) to shake thoroughly, = 。dzól-ba ('dzol ba).

1. point, top, rdo-rjei (rdo rje'i) of the dor-je (rdo rje) Dom; summit, of the Rirab and some other mountains S.O. and elsewh.; zom-kóg (zom kog) dull, simple, stupid, Sch. — 2. cave Sch., brag-zóm (brag zom) rock-cavern.

1. sup. of zó-ba (zo ba), bzó-ba (bzo ba) Sch. — 2. sbst. the weapons employed in combating the evil spirits in the gtór-ma (gtor ma), such as knife, sword, sling, bow and arrows etc.; zor-k̄a (zor kha) the fore- or front-part, the edge, of the weapons directed against the demons, zór-k̄u p̌éṅ-pa (zor khu 'phen pa) Cs.: to fling those weapons a gainst the spirits.

sickle, zór-bas rṅá-ba (zor bas rnga ba) Mil., gcód-pa (gcod pa) Cs. to cut with a sickle, zór-lcè (zor lce) sickle-blade; zor-čúṅ (zor chung) small, zor-čén (zor chen) large sickle, scythe, though in Tibet as yet hardly known; zor-rtúl (zor rtul) blunt, dull, zor-rnón (zor rnon) sharp sickle; zór-bu (zor bu) = zor-čúṅ (zor chung).

deceit, fraud, imposture, falsehood, zol-zóg byéd-pa (zol zog byed pa), W. *r̀o-c̀e'* (*ro che'*), to deceive, impose on, e.g in traffic Thgy., zól-zog-c̀an (zol zog can) deceitful, fraudulent, zol(-zog)-méd (zol zog med) without deceit, free from guile, artless Mil.
1. for zlá-ba (zla ba). — 2. for zlá-bo (zla bo).

I. sbst. 1. prov. zla (zla), moon, frq.; mkai zlá-ba (mkha'i zla ba) celestial moon Lex., to distinguish it from 2. dús-kyi zlá-ba (dus kyi zla ba) temporal moon or month, zlá-ba gc̀ig (zla ba gcig), B, W., *da c̀ig* (*da cig*) C., one month; *zlá-ba ma 。kor šog* (*zla ba ma 'khor shog*) come before the end of the month Sch.; zlá-ba tsáṅ-du nyé-bas (zla ba tshang du nye bas) towards the expi-
